codeReview.summary: The feature implementation covers the requested behavior: tool_calling responses are parsed via parse_tool_calls() before check(), parsed calls are logged to CSV via parsed_tool_calls, CLI summary now includes tool-calling subcategory breakdowns, and the README-table annotation was updated to 81 total problems. Added integration tests provide strong coverage for runner dispatch, parser/check flow, CSV output, and problem-count assertions.

issues.description: New tests use hardcoded absolute filesystem paths to load scripts (also at line 1156), which reduces portability across environments/workspaces. Prefer deriving paths from the repo root or test file location.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-worker skill mandates TDD (write failing tests before implementation), but this run implemented production code first and added tests afterward while still reporting followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md Step 3 says "Write Tests First (TDD)"; transcript skeleton shows edits to mtb/quality_benchmarks/run_quality_benchmark.py and scripts/* before test additions in tests/test_runner_integration.py.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Tool-calling subcategory derivation via name prefixes (ts_/aa_/mt_/ec_/fc_) appears implicit in problem naming and not explicitly documented in shared docs, causing extra discovery work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton includes exploratory grep/python steps to infer subcategory prefixes; AGENTS.md and .factory/library/architecture.md describe tool-calling category but not the prefix-to-subcategory mapping used by the summary.

summary: Reviewed feature runner-tool-call-support (commit 6a1249f): implementation satisfies core requirements with no blocking defects found. One non-blocking test portability issue (hardcoded absolute paths) was noted, along with shared-state observations about TDD procedure adherence and undocumented subcategory prefix mapping.
